headline: PSI log retention/rotation tool — pruning by --keep-days or --keep-rows; default DRY-RUN; atomic write + always-on backup
problem_statement: The canonical PSI log at /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log accumulates indefinitely (per ω-network-5: 346 rows over 4h37m → ~75/h). At sustained ops it grows into MB territory, slowing per-host calibration scanning by tool/psi_calibrate_emit.hexa (which sorts every row in awk for percentiles). No rotation/retention policy existed. ω-network-7 closes the gap with a sweeper-safe .hexa pruner.
fix_summary: Added tool/psi_log_retention.hexa: a default-DRY-RUN retention tool that prunes old rows from a TSV PSI log according to one of two policies (--keep-days D | --keep-rows N), preserves the file's commented header lines, and writes atomically (tmp + rename). A timestamped backup copy is always created before --apply. Removed-row preview is shown in dry-run mode. Both --json and --quiet output modes supported. Standardized sentinel (raw 80) emitted in all paths.

raw_compliance: raw 73 admissibility (mutually-exclusive flags fail-fast with fix message)
raw 77 append-only-respect (chronological order preserved; only suffix kept)
raw 80 sentinel: __PSI_LOG_RETENTION__ <DRY_RUN|APPLIED|SKIP> kept=K removed=R total=T policy=<keep-days|keep-rows> param=P backup=PATH

design_decisions: Default DRY-RUN — every invocation without --apply produces only stdout text + sentinel; the file is never mutated. This makes the tool safe to wire into cron without risking data loss.
Atomic write — buffer the kept content into /tmp/psi_log_retention.tmp.<pid>.<ts>, then `mv -f` to the target. If the process is killed mid-write, the tmp file is orphaned but the original is intact.
Always-on backup — `cp -p` original to <log>.bak.<unix_ts> before any rewrite. The backup directory's writability is probed even on dry-run so misconfigured permissions surface before they matter.

Mutually-exclusive policies fail fast (exit 1) — explicit `fix:` line tells the operator what to change. Using BOTH flags together would be ambiguous (which one wins?), so we refuse rather than guess.

Suffix-keep only — we never select a non-contiguous subset. For --keep-days, we walk the log oldest→newest and find the first row whose ts ≥ now-D*86400; everything from that index onward is kept. This honors raw 77 append-only ordering and matches the calibrator's expectation that rows arrive chronologically.

Header preservation — we capture #-prefixed lines from the top of the file as a contiguous header block and re-emit them verbatim in the rewrite. The current production log has no header, but tools that prepend a schema comment (raw 77 history) round-trip safely.
ISO8601 epoch parsing reuses the BSD-first / GNU-fallback `iso_to_epoch` pattern from tool/dormancy_wake_tick.hexa for portability between macOS dev and Linux remote hosts.
Cleanup of orphan tmp file on rename failure — explicit `rm -f` to avoid /tmp polluton if a future filesystem-level rename ever fails.

constraints_honored.no_parallel_session_collision: OK — did not touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own, scripts/bin/hexa_remote.
constraints_honored.real_log_untouched_during_validation: OK — all 6 fixture cases used /tmp/psi_test_*.log paths. /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log was never the target of --apply during validation; pre/post wc -l confirmed (481 lines unchanged).
constraints_honored.no_apply_against_production_log: OK — no operator-side --apply call against /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log issued.
constraints_honored.no_anima_commit: OK — closure JSON is UNTRACKED at /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/.
constraints_honored.history_ops_pin_sha: N/A — no rewrites; clean forward commit on top of pinned HEAD 5832b468.

fixtures.actual_sentinel: __PSI_LOG_RETENTION__ DRY_RUN kept=24 removed=76 total=100 policy=keep-days param=7
__PSI_LOG_RETENTION__ DRY_RUN kept=50 removed=50 total=100 policy=keep-rows param=50
__PSI_LOG_RETENTION__ SKIP kept=0 removed=0 total=0 policy=keep-days param=7 backup=- reason=no_data_rows path=/tmp/psi_test_empty.log
__PSI_LOG_RETENTION__ SKIP kept=0 removed=0 total=0 policy=keep-days param=7 backup=- reason=log_missing path=/tmp/psi_test_does_not_exist.log

fixtures.expected: file shrinks 100→30 lines; backup file exists at /tmp/psi_test_synth100.log.bak.<ts>
fixtures.round_trip_calibrator_safe: OK — psi_calibrate_emit.hexa --json against pruned file: rows=30, parses cleanly

fixtures.actual_stderr: psi_log_retention: error: --keep-days and --keep-rows are mutually exclusive\n  fix: pass exactly one policy flag

production_log_untouched.delta_explanation: Real log accumulates from concurrent hexa_remote calls; no .bak.* siblings created — confirming this tool never wrote to it.

validation.json_output_smoke: OK — --json --quiet produces single-line JSON object with the documented field set

follow_ups: Wire into a daily LaunchAgent (similar plist pattern to com.nexus.psi_calibration_refresh) running --keep-days 7 (dry-run for first 1-2 weeks of ops, then --apply once retention shape is confirmed safe).
Consider adding a --max-bytes ceiling as a third policy (e.g. shrink to fit ≤2MB) for hosts with constrained /tmp.
Optional: emit __PSI_LOG_RETENTION__ APPLIED rows into bridge_health_history.tsv (post-ω-bridge-15 wiring) so the anomaly detector can spot abnormal retention churn.
